title: dryopteris cambrensis subsp. cambrensis
intro: Dryopteris cambrensis subsp. cambrensis, also known as Welsh scaly male fern, is a species of fern endemic to Wales, characterized by its scaly rhizome and lance-shaped fronds. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can help to soothe and calm irritated skin and mucous membranes. In terms of health benefits, the plant is believed to have antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, making it potentially useful in the treatment of various infections and diseases. The herbal preparations of Welsh scaly male fern typically involve drying the fronds or rhizome, which can then be used in teas, infusions, or topical applications to harness its medicinal properties.
